sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Shortness of breath
History of Present Illness
The patient reports a two-week history of increasing difficulty sleeping, with a sensation of choking a few minutes after lying down. She describes this as an inability to catch her breath. She reports difficulty with weight gain, estimating a ten-pound increase over the past month. She admits to enjoying a large party last weekend with a lot of food and sweets, and reports not consistently adhering to her prescribed diet control plan for diabetes. She reports feeling 'crazy' and unable to figure out why, and admits to drinking very little. She has been taking metformin intermittently and is unsure how much salt is in the foods she consumes. She reports some morning cough with a small amount of sputum. She denies fever or chest pain.
Findings
Physical exam reveals mild edema in the ankles, with 1+ pitting edema. Lung exam demonstrates crackles in the bases and intermittent wheezing. Heart exam reveals a two out of six systolic ejection murmur. Neurological exam is normal. Vital signs are stable.
Assessment
The patient presents with an exacerbation of congestive heart failure, complicated by underlying diabetes and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
Plan

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: I am going to increase your lasix dose from twenty milligrams to sixty milligrams for the next four days. Please monitor your weight daily. I'm going to have you use your albuterol and atrovent inhalers as needed. I'm also going to order a repeat hemoglobin a1c test. I'm going to prescribe naprosyn and flexeril for your back pain. I'd like to schedule a follow-up appointment in approximately two days to reassess your symptoms and adjust your treatment plan as needed. I'm going to provide you with a refill for your lasix and albuterol inhalers.
